english_forum.title: Move to New Folder: create a folder and move a file or folder in one flow
english_forum.author: Fred_V
english_forum.content_html:
Hi, fellow Obsidianers,

Whenever I wanted a feature that didnŌĆÖt exist in core, I could always find a plugin for it because this community is great. Now the time has come for me to contribute a little too by making my first plugin: Move to New Folder. It helps move a file or folder into a newly created folder in a single flow.

It currently supports:

  • moving a file to a new folder

  • moving a folder to a new folder

  • choosing the parent folder from a searchable picker

The plugin is in beta and can be installed through BRAT:

  • d0d1/obsidian-move-to-new-folder

Feedback, bug reports, and suggestions are very welcome here or on GitHub via Issues and Discussions.

# I am submitting a new Community Plugin

- [x] I attest that I have done my best to deliver a high-quality plugin, am proud of the code I have written, and would recommend it to others. I commit to maintaining the plugin and being responsive to bug reports. If I am no longer able to maintain it, I will make reasonable efforts to find a successor maintainer or withdraw the plugin from the directory.

## Repo URL

<!--- Paste a link to your repo here for easy access -->
Link to my plugin: https://github.com/mirkostanic/obsidian-lego

## Release Checklist
- [x] I have tested the plugin on
  - [x]  Windows
  - [x]  macOS
  - [x]  Linux
  - [ ]  Android _(if applicable)_
  - [ ]  iOS _(if applicable)_
- [x] My GitHub release contains all required files (as individual files, not just in the source.zip / source.tar.gz)
  - [x] `main.js`
  - [x] `manifest.json`
  - [x] `styles.css` _(optional)_
- [x] GitHub release name matches the exact version number specified in my manifest.json (_**Note:** Use the exact version number, don't include a prefix `v`_)
- [x] The `id` in my `manifest.json` matches the `id` in the `community-plugins.json` file.
- [x] My README.md describes the plugin's purpose and provides clear usage instructions.
- [x] I have read the developer policies at https://docs.obsidian.md/Developer+policies, and have assessed my plugin's adherence to these policies.
- [x] I have read the tips in https://docs.obsidian.md/Plugins/Releasing/Plugin+guidelines and have self-reviewed my plugin to avoid these common pitfalls.
- [x] I have added a license in the LICENSE file.
- [x] My project respects and is compatible with the original license of any code from other plugins that I'm using.
      I have given proper attribution to these other projects in my `README.md`.

github_opened.title: Add theme: Cloud Dancer
github_opened.author: Pierry
github_opened.body:
## Add Community Theme

**Theme name:** Cloud Dancer
**Author:** Pierry Borges
**Repository:** https://github.com/Pierry/obsidian-cloud-dancer-theme

### Description

A warm, elegant theme inspired by **Pantone Cloud Dancer (#F0EBE3)** ŌĆö a soft off-white that evokes calm and clarity.

### Features
- Light mode built around Cloud Dancer's warm off-white palette
- Dark mode with deep warm charcoal tones
- Warm terracotta accent colors
- Clean typography with thoughtful heading hierarchy

### Checklist
- [x] Theme repo is public
- [x] `manifest.json` exists with required fields
- [x] `theme.css` exists at root
- [x] Both light and dark modes supported
- [x] MIT License included
- [x] Screenshot included

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

github_opened.title: Add marginalia plugin
github_opened.author: belvisogi
github_opened.body:
## Plugin submission

**Plugin ID:** marginalia
**Plugin name:** marginalia
**Repository:** [hale-bopp-data/marginalia](https://github.com/hale-bopp-data/marginalia)
**Release:** [v1.1.1](https://github.com/hale-bopp-data/marginalia/releases/tag/v1.1.1)

### Description

Markdown vault quality scanner for Obsidian, academics, and documentation teams. Calls the marginalia Python CLI to scan your vault for quality issues, suggest related links, and auto-fix frontmatter.

### Checklist

- [x] `manifest.json` has all required fields (id, name, version, minAppVersion, description, author, authorUrl, isDesktopOnly)
- [x] GitHub Release exists with `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`
- [x] Repository has a LICENSE (MIT)
- [x] Plugin has a README
- [x] No duplicate plugin ID

github_opened.title: Add theme: Cyberpunk
github_opened.author: nxxxsooo
github_opened.body:
## Add Community Theme: Cyberpunk

**Repo:** https://github.com/nxxxsooo/obsidian-cyberpunk-theme

A neon-soaked, glow-heavy dark theme for Obsidian. Ported from [channingwalton's Typora Cyberpunk theme](https://github.com/channingwalton/typora-themes).

### Features
- Neon glow headings (H1ŌĆōH6 each with different neon color + multi-layer text-shadow)
- Heading prefixes (`Ō¢Ė` `//` `>` `::`) in contrasting neon
- H1 flicker animation
- Matrix-green code blocks with `[ CODE ]` badge
- Rainbow gradient horizontal rules with glow
- Cyberpunk grid background overlay
- Animated glitch bar on right edge
- Orbitron sci-fi font for headings
- Full coverage: callouts, tables, checkboxes, graph, nav, command palette
- Works in both live preview and reading view

### Checklist
- [x] Theme repo contains `manifest.json` and `theme.css`
- [x] `manifest.json` has `name`, `version`, `minAppVersion`, `author`, `authorUrl`
- [x] Theme repo has a `screenshot.png`
- [x] Theme repo has a `LICENSE`
- [x] Only `dark` mode declared (theme is dark-only)

github_opened.body:

- [x] I attest that I have done my best to deliver a high-quality plugin, am proud of the code I have written, and would recommend it to others. I commit to maintaining the plugin and being responsive to bug reports. If I am no longer able to maintain it, I will make reasonable efforts to find a successor maintainer or withdraw the plugin from the directory.

## Repo URL

Link to my plugin: https://github.com/hqy2020/linkpilot-obsidian

Release URL: https://github.com/hqy2020/linkpilot-obsidian/releases/tag/0.1.0

## Release Checklist
- [x] I have tested the plugin on
  - [ ] Windows
  - [x] macOS
  - [ ] Linux
  - [ ] Android _(if applicable)_
  - [ ] iOS _(if applicable)_
- [x] My GitHub release contains all required files (as individual files, not just in the source.zip / source.tar.gz)
  - [x] `main.js`
  - [x] `manifest.json`
  - [x] `styles.css` _(optional)_
- [x] GitHub release name matches the exact version number specified in my manifest.json.
- [x] The `id` in my `manifest.json` matches the `id` in the `community-plugins.json` file.
- [x] My `README.md` describes the plugin purpose and provides clear usage instructions.
- [x] I have read the developer policies at https://docs.obsidian.md/Developer+policies and assessed my plugin against them.
- [x] I have read the tips in https://docs.obsidian.md/Plugins/Releasing/Plugin+guidelines and self-reviewed the plugin against those common pitfalls.
- [x] I have added a license in the `LICENSE` file.
- [x] My project respects and is compatible with the original license of any code from other plugins that I am using, and I have added attribution in the `README.md`.

## Notes

- Desktop-only plugin.
- Manual runtime verification is completed on macOS.
- Optional network and PostgreSQL usage are explicitly disclosed in the README and remain user-configured, opt-in capabilities.
